title: VGRO ETF Review 2026: 80/20 Portfolio Canada
description: Complete 2026 review of the Vanguard Growth ETF Portfolio (VGRO). Explore the 80/20 allocation, MER, performance, and how it compares to XGRO and ZGRO.

mainEntity.name: What is the VGRO expense ratio?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Vanguard Growth ETF Portfolio (VGRO) has an expense ratio of 0.24%. This means for every $10,000 invested, you pay $24 per year in management fees.

Both VGRO and XGRO offer similar 80/20 asset allocations. XGRO has a slightly lower MER at 0.20% compared to VGRO's 0.24%. The best choice depends on your brokerage's free ETF purchase list and minor preference differences in underlying holdings.

mainEntity.name: What is the dividend yield of VGRO?

acceptedAnswer.text: As of 2026, VGRO has a trailing 12-month dividend yield of approximately 1.80%. Yields fluctuate based on the distributions of the underlying Vanguard index funds.
An 80/20 portfolio like VGRO allocates roughly 80% of its assets to equities (stocks) for growth, and 20% to fixed income (bonds) to reduce volatility and provide stability.

Vanguard automatically rebalances VGRO's underlying holdings on an ongoing basis to maintain the target 80% equity and 20% fixed income allocation, saving investors the time and trading fees of manual rebalancing.
